title: Tweak info time format
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

This change touches up the way `podman info` represented the uptime.  Before it was:

```
  os: linux
  uptime: 464h26m52.62s
```
With this change it is:
```
  os: linux
  uptime: 464h 26m 52.62s (Approximately 19.33 days)

(on a newly created machine)
  os: linux
  uptime: 20m 2.53s
```

As I believe @mheon mentioned in a previous review, coming up with an exact number for the days is tricky at best due to a day being 23, 24, or 25 hours long.  Plus the odd leap second, minute, or what have you.  So I chickened out and went with the approximation.  It's just an in the neighborhood thing and I find it handy myself.

This PR originally had a change to the CLI help to remove a duplicate 'build', @sjenning beat me to the punch on that one in #413
